docs.title: Open Private Markets Glossary
docs.text: A shared reference vocabulary for private markets. Work in progress.

docs.title: Firm types
docs.text: Terms describing kinds of firms that participate in private markets.

docs.title: Allocator
docs.text: An allocator allocates capital to private market funds and managers on behalf of underlying beneficiaries or its own balance sheet.

docs.title: Fund Manager
docs.text: A fund manager is a firm that raises capital from investors into pooled investment vehicles and deploys that capital into private market assets according to a defined strategy. The fund manager typically takes the general-partner role in the fund structures it operates.

docs.title: General Partner
docs.text: The general partner (GP) manages the fund, makes investment decisions, and typically bears unlimited liability in a limited partnership structure.

docs.title: Fund structures
docs.text: Terms describing how private market funds are organized legally and economically.

docs.title: Closed-end fund
docs.text: A closed-end fund draws down committed capital over a defined investment period, holds assets until exit, and distributes proceeds within a finite fund life.
